content.thinking:
Now I have a good understanding of the codebase. Let me analyze the issue:

**FRG-212: Brainstorm mode: continuous recording with real-time transcript**

The description says:
- No VAD in brainstorm mode — record continuously until Stop is pressed ✅ (already implemented via silenceTimer only applied to quick mode)
- Show real-time transcript while speaking (reference Brainwave repo for implementation) - the live transcript is showing already, but the issue says this needs improvement
- On stop, produce both **raw transcript** and a **cleaned readable version**

Looking at the existing code:
1. Brainstorm mode already records continuously (no VAD) ✅


2. Real-time transcript displays via `liveTranscript` and `liveText` ✅
3. On stop, currently just shows raw transcript in transcript bar

**What's missing:**
- The live transcript for brainstorm mode should be more visible/styled differently (auto-scroll as text accumulates)
- On stop, need to produce **both raw transcript AND a cleaned readable version**

The key new feature is: **on stop, produce both raw transcript and a cleaned readable version**. I need to examine the `finishRecording()` function to see where I can add logic to generate both versions of the transcript.

Currently it just displays the raw text in the transcript bar, but for brainstorm mode I should show both the original capture and a processed version that fixes capitalization, adds proper punctuation, removes filler words like "um" and "uh", and cleans up duplicate words.

Looking at the transcript bar structure, I need to decide whether to toggle between the two versions or display them side-by-side. I'm leaning toward showing both simultaneously so the user can see what was captured versus what got cleaned up, and I'll need to extend the HTML layout to accommodate this dual display.

Now I'm mapping out the implementation work: I'll need to add a cleaning function, update the transcript bar markup to include separate sections for raw and cleaned text, style the new layout appropriately, and modify the JavaScript to detect brainstorm mode and populate both versions when recording finishes.

For the live transcript during brainstorm sessions, I should make sure it scrolls automatically as new content comes in. I'm looking at the existing live transcript structure and CSS to add overflow handling and scroll behavior.

Now I'm thinking through how to clean up the transcript text itself—I need a function that trims whitespace, capitalizes sentences properly, detects when punctuation is missing after pauses, and removes filler words to make the output cleaner and more readable.

For the UI, I'll display both versions side by side when the user stops recording: the cleaned transcript prominently at the top with a label, and the raw version below it in a muted style so they can compare. The Send button will submit the cleaned version by default.

Now I'm structuring the HTML to support this—adding hidden sections for the cleaned and raw transcripts that only appear in brainstorm mode, with labels to distinguish them.

Setting up the JavaScript to reference these elements and defining a function that strips out common filler words and verbal tics... Still writing the transcript handler... I need to make sure `finishRecording` passes the mode to `showTranscriptBar` so the cleaned text gets displayed with the right styling. For brainstorm sessions with longer transcripts, I'm adding a scrollable container with auto-scroll functionality to keep the live text visible as it updates.

Writing component styles... Finishing the CSS styles...

Now I'm thinking through the actual feature logic — when the user stops speaking, I need to show both the cleaned and raw versions of the transcript. The cleaned version should be the primary display with a label, and the raw text below it as a reference. The Send button will submit the cleaned version since that's the polished, readable output.

I also need to update the `hideTranscriptBar` function to properly reset both the cleaned and raw transcript sections when hiding the bar, clearing out any previous text and hiding those elements.

Now I'm refining the HTML structure to keep things organized — the cleaned label and transcript text show in brainstorm mode, while the raw section with its own label stays hidden until needed, and the action buttons remain at the bottom.

Building the markup... I'm thinking through the edge cases—if the cleaned text matches the raw text, both sections will display identically which is fine, and the feature should handle short text and wake-triggered recordings appropriately. Now I'm ready to implement this, and I notice the issue is already marked as "Dev in Progress" so I don't need to update that status.

I'll use the branch naming convention `feature/frg-212-brainstorm-dual-transcript` instead of the one in the issue. For the `cleanTranscript` function, I'm building logic to strip out common filler words and phrases...

Writing transcript cleaning logic... I'm finishing up the text cleaning by capitalizing the first letter and any letters after sentence-ending punctuation, then ensuring the text ends with a period if it's missing one. But I'm realizing the filler word removal might be too aggressive—words like "actually" and "literally" aren't always just filler, so I should probably keep only the more obvious ones like "um," "uh," and "er."

Refining the approach to focus on obvious filler sounds, capitalization fixes, punctuation normalization, and whitespace cleanup...

Still writing cleanup logic... I'm implementing the text cleaning function and now moving through the file changes systematically. For the HTML transcript bar, I need to add a "Cleaned" label and raw transcript section that toggle visibility based on brainstorm mode, restructuring the existing transcript display to accommodate both cleaned and raw versions.

Adding CSS styles for the transcript labels... Writing component styles and JavaScript updates...

content.text:
Now I have a clear picture of the codebase. Let me understand the issue:

- **FRG-212**: Brainstorm mode needs to show real-time transcript with auto-scroll, and on stop produce **both** a raw transcript and a cleaned readable version.

The existing code already has continuous recording (no VAD in brainstorm), but is missing:
1. Live transcript auto-scrolling for long brainstorm sessions  
2. Dual output on stop: raw + cleaned transcript

Let me create the branch and implement:

input.command:
git add app.js index.html style.css && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(FRG-212): brainstorm dual transcript — real-time scroll + cleaned output

- Add cleanTranscript() to strip filler sounds (um/uh/er), fix
  capitalisation, and add terminal punctuation to produce a readable
  version of the raw dictation.
- showTranscriptBar() now accepts the recording mode; in brainstorm
  mode it renders a "Cleaned" section (sent on Save) above a
  collateral "Raw" section so the user can see both outputs.
- Live transcript panel gains .brainstorm-mode CSS class while
  recording, capping its height to 130 px with overflow scroll.
  updateLiveText() auto-scrolls to the latest text in brainstorm mode.
- hideTranscriptBar() resets the new raw/cleaned DOM nodes on dismiss.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

input.command:
gh pr create \
  --title "feat(FRG-212): brainstorm dual transcript — real-time scroll + cleaned output" \
  --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

- **Real-time transcript auto-scroll**: while Brainstorm is recording, the live transcript panel is capped at 130 px and auto-scrolls to keep the latest spoken text visible.
- **Cleaned transcript on stop**: after stopping a Brainstorm session, the transcript bar shows two sections:
  - **Cleaned** (top, sent when the user taps Send) — strips spoken filler sounds (`um`, `uh`, `er`, `hmm`), fixes capitalisation, and adds a terminal period for readability.
  - **Raw** (below a divider) — the verbatim dictation, for reference.
- Quick mode is unchanged: single transcript, no cleaning step.

## Test plan

- [ ] Start a Brainstorm recording; dictate more than ~3 lines — confirm the live transcript panel scrolls automatically
- [ ] Stop the recording; confirm both "Cleaned" and "Raw" sections appear in the transcript bar
- [ ] Verify the Cleaned section strips `um`/`uh`, capitalises the first word, and ends with a period
- [ ] Verify Send posts the cleaned text to the webhook
- [ ] Verify Discard clears both sections
- [ ] Start a Quick recording; confirm only one section (no labels, no raw) appears after stopping
- [ ] Wake-word-triggered recording still auto-sends without showing the transcript bar

Closes [FRG-212](https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-212/brainstorm-mode-continuous-recording-with-real-time-transcript)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF

content.text:
---

## Status
DONE

## What Changed
- **`index.html`** — added `#transcript-cleaned-label` (label shown above cleaned text in brainstorm) and `#transcript-raw-wrap` / `#transcript-raw-text` (raw transcript section, hidden in quick mode)
- **`style.css`** — added `.transcript-section-label`, `.transcript-raw-wrap`, `.transcript-raw-text` styles for the dual-transcript layout; added `.live-transcript.brainstorm-mode` for a 130 px max-height scrollable live transcript
- **`app.js`** — new DOM refs for the three new elements; `cleanTranscript()` strips filler sounds (`um/uh/er/hmm`), fixes capitalisation, adds terminal period; `showTranscriptBar(text, mode)` now renders cleaned+raw sections in brainstorm, single section in quick; `hideTranscriptBar()` resets both; `setRecordingUI()` toggles `.brainstorm-mode` class; `updateLiveText()` auto-scrolls the live panel during brainstorm

## Key Decisions
- **Send posts the cleaned version** — cleaned text goes into `#transcript-text` (what Send reads), raw is shown below as reference. This matches the "readable" intent without needing a second button.
- **Conservative filler removal** — only obvious non-semantic sounds (`um/uh/er/hmm`) are stripped; content words like "actually" or "like" are preserved to avoid accidentally altering meaning.

## PR / Branch
- Branch: `feature/frg-212-brainstorm-dual-transcript`
- PR: https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/hey-chill/pull/6

---

## Status
DONE

## What Changed
- **`index.html`** — added `#transcript-cleaned-label` (label shown above cleaned text in brainstorm) and `#transcript-raw-wrap` / `#transcript-raw-text` (raw transcript section, hidden in quick mode)
- **`style.css`** — added `.transcript-section-label`, `.transcript-raw-wrap`, `.transcript-raw-text` styles for the dual-transcript layout; added `.live-transcript.brainstorm-mode` for a 130 px max-height scrollable live transcript
- **`app.js`** — new DOM refs for the three new elements; `cleanTranscript()` strips filler sounds (`um/uh/er/hmm`), fixes capitalisation, adds terminal period; `showTranscriptBar(text, mode)` now renders cleaned+raw sections in brainstorm, single section in quick; `hideTranscriptBar()` resets both; `setRecordingUI()` toggles `.brainstorm-mode` class; `updateLiveText()` auto-scrolls the live panel during brainstorm

## Key Decisions
- **Send posts the cleaned version** — cleaned text goes into `#transcript-text` (what Send reads), raw is shown below as reference. This matches the "readable" intent without needing a second button.
- **Conservative filler removal** — only obvious non-semantic sounds (`um/uh/er/hmm`) are stripped; content words like "actually" or "like" are preserved to avoid accidentally altering meaning.

## PR / Branch
- Branch: `feature/frg-212-brainstorm-dual-transcript`
- PR: https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/hey-chill/pull/6
